title: Today's AI Brief: The Download
summary: Unlocking lithium, controlling Ebola, and a new era for AI Search

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. Let's dive into some interesting developments in the world of technology. First up, a new extraction process for lithium could be a game-changer. Lithium is a crucial component in batteries, and this new method might make it easier to access. According to a recent article in Technology Review, this could unlock the world's lithium supply.

The current extraction process for lithium is complex and often inefficient. This new method aims to simplify things, making it more cost-effective and environmentally friendly. My read is that this could have significant implications for the battery industry and, by extension, electric vehicles and renewable energy.
A New Era for AI Search

Google is bringing its advanced model capabilities to Search with new AI features. This means you can now use agents just by asking a question. It seems like a natural progression, but I think it's interesting to see how this will change the way we interact with search results. No more sifting through pages of links – just ask, and get a direct answer.

On a related note, AI is being used to control Ebola. It appears that researchers are exploring ways to use AI to better understand and combat the virus. I find it fascinating to see how technology is being applied to solve some of humanity's most pressing problems.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
